body: PT PP Presisi Tbk (PPRE) reported a net profit of IDR 7.7 billion in the first nine months of 2024, an 80.7% year-on-year decline from IDR 39.4 billion in the same period last year, due to decreasing profit margins despite a 5% year-on-year revenue increase to IDR 2.72 trillion. The company's profitability was burdened by rising costs, including high revenue costs, operating expenses, impairment losses, finance costs, and final tax expenses, resulting in total assets of IDR 7.79 trillion and total equity of IDR 3.39 trillion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT PP Presisi TBK's net profit plunges 80.7% to IDR 7.7 billion in the first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Tbk (TRIM) reported a net profit of IDR 151.73 billion in the first nine months of 2024, a 53% year-on-year increase from IDR 99.16 billion in the same period last year, driven by a 20.7% increase in operating revenue to IDR 622.86 billion. The company's investment manager services, brokerage commissions, and underwriting fees were key contributors to its revenue growth, with total assets reaching IDR 3.83 trillion and equity at IDR 1.37 trillion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Tbk sees 53% year-on-year increase in net profit to IDR 151.73 billion in first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Mulia Boga Raya Tbk (no ticker provided) reported a 122.86% year-on-year profit growth to IDR 113.52 billion in Q3 2024, driven by a significant improvement in profit before taxes and gross profit, which increased by 120% and 52.16% respectively. The company's net sales also surged 28.22% year-on-year to IDR 939.18 billion, with block cheese sales being the largest contributor, reaching IDR 719.37 billion.
title: Prochiz Cheese Manufacturer PT Mulia Boga Raya Sees 122.86% Profit Growth in Q3 2024.

body: PT DCI Indonesia Tbk (DCII) reported a net profit of IDR 449.5 billion in the first nine months of 2024, a 21.3% year-over-year increase from IDR 370.43 billion in 9M 2023, driven by a 14.6% revenue growth to IDR 1.1 trillion. The company's colocation business line remained the main revenue driver, generating IDR 1.03 trillion, with total assets reaching IDR 3.98 trillion and cash and cash equivalents of IDR 307.7 billion as of September 30, 2024.
title: DCI Indonesia's net profit surges 21.3% to IDR 449.5 billion in the first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Erajaya Swasembada Tbk (ERAA) reported a 60.12% year-over-year increase in net profit to IDR 791.16 billion in Q3 2024, driven by a 13.52% increase in sales to IDR 48.60 trillion, with cell phone and tablet sales accounting for 81.10% of total revenue. The company's gross profit and operating profit also rose by 2.12% and 36.83%, respectively, with sales growth seen in computer and electronic devices, as well as accessories, while operator products' sales declined.
title: PT Erajaya Swasembada reports 60.12% net profit growth in Q3 2024 driven by strong cell phone and tablet sales.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) remained stagnant at 7,770, with a negative trend similar to the pattern seen in the last two weeks, causing a decline in large market cap stocks such as PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK, PT Chandra Asri Pacific TBK, and PT Astra International TBK. Meanwhile, some top stocks saw positive shifts, including PT Amman Mineral Internasional TBK, PT Telkom Indonesia TBK, and PT Adaro Energy Indonesia TBK, with indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL also experiencing declines.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Remains Stagnant with Decline in Major Stocks and Indices.

body: PT Fap Agri Tbk (FAPA) received a dividend income of IDR 47.5 billion from its subsidiary PT Tirta Madu Sawit Jaya, where it holds a 95% direct ownership stake. This is not the first dividend income received from PT Tirta Madu Sawit Jaya, as FAPA also recorded IDR 47.5 billion in August 2024, and additionally collected dividends from PT Riau Agung Karya Abadi and PT Tirta Madu Sawit Jaya in June-July 2024, totaling IDR 116.85 billion.
title: PT Fap Agri Tbk announces dividend income of IDR 47.5 billion from subsidiary PT Tirta Madu Sawit Jaya.

body: PT Delta Wibawa Bersama increased its stake in PT Lima Dua Lima Tiga TBK to 58.09% by acquiring 130.02 million shares, while other investors, including PT Pangan Sejahtera Investama and PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala, also made notable purchases in various companies. Meanwhile, foreign investors, such as UOB Kay Hian Ltd and OCBC Sec Pte Ltd, decreased their holdings in companies like PT Golden Flower TBK and PT Nusa Konstruksi Enjiniring TBK.
title: Major Shareholders and Investors Make Significant Transactions in Indonesian Companies

body: PT Rukun Raharja Tbk (RAJA) has secured a credit facility agreement worth over USD 44.8 million from PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), comprising a term loan, bank-guaranteed loan, and investment credit facility. The funds will be used for investment purposes by the company and its subsidiary, with RAJA's stock price surging 13.44% since the beginning of the year and 80.21% over the past year, reaching IDR 1,730 per share.
title: PT Rukun Raharja's subsidiary secures USD 44.8 million credit facility from PT Bank Mandiri for investment and project funding.

body: PT Garuda Maintenance Facility Aero Asia Tbk (GMFI) is planning to hold a rights issue of 11.73 billion shares, equivalent to 41.7% of its total issued and paid-up capital, to improve its equity, which currently has a deficiency of USD 298.12 million. The rights issue is expected to boost GMFI's total assets to IDR 418.28 billion (USD 25.47 billion) and reduce its equity deficiency to USD 270.6 million, with proceeds to be allocated towards operational costs and aircraft spare parts.
title: Garuda Maintenance Facility Aero Asia to Raise Capital through Rights Issue of 11.73 Billion Shares

body: PT Toyota Astra Financial Services (TAFS) is set to issue a IDR 1 trillion shelf-registration bond, consisting of two series with interest rates of 6.35% and 6.60% per annum, with a listing date of November 22, 2024. In the first half of 2024, TAFS reported revenue of IDR 2.15 trillion, up from IDR 1.80 trillion in H1 2023, driven by general financing, operation leasing, and other segments.
title: PT Toyota Astra Financial Services to Issue IDR 1 Trillion Bond in Two Series with Different Tenors and Interest Rates.
